   17 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   18 | //| Class CSonicRWavePattern                                         |
   19 | //| Purpose: Elliott Wave pattern recognition and analysis           |
   20 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   21 | class CSonicRWavePattern
   22 | {
   23 | public:
   24 |     // Structures and enums are now in Shared_DataStructures.mqh
   25 |     
   26 | private:
   27 |     // Core dependencies
   28 |     CLogger*         m_pLogger;
   29 |     CIndicators*     m_pIndicators;
   30 |     CSymbolInfo*     m_pSymbolInfo;
   31 |     
   32 |     // Configuration
   33 |     int              m_lookbackPeriod;      // Lookback period for analysis
   34 |     int              m_minWaveLength;       // Minimum wave length in bars
   35 |     double           m_fibTolerance;        // Fibonacci ratio tolerance
   36 |     double           m_strengthThreshold;   // Minimum strength threshold
   37 |     
   38 |     // Wave data
   39 |     SWavePattern     m_currentPattern;      // Current wave pattern
   40 |     SWavePoint       m_swingPoints[];       // Swing points history
   41 |     int              m_swingPointCount;     // Number of swing points
   42 |     
   43 |     // Fibonacci levels
   44 |     double           m_fibLevels[13];       // Standard Fibonacci levels
   45 |     
   46 | public:
   47 |     // Constructor & Destructor
   48 |                      CSonicRWavePattern();
   49 |                     ~CSonicRWavePattern();
   50 | 
   51 |     // Initialization
   52 |     bool Initialize(CLogger* pLogger, CIndicators* pIndicators, CSymbolInfo* pSymbolInfo);
   53 |     void Deinitialize();
   54 |     
   55 |     // Configuration
   56 |     void SetLookbackPeriod(int period) { m_lookbackPeriod = period; }
   57 |     void SetMinWaveLength(int length) { m_minWaveLength = length; }
   58 |     void SetFibTolerance(double tolerance) { m_fibTolerance = tolerance; }
   59 |     void SetStrengthThreshold(double threshold) { m_strengthThreshold = threshold; }
   60 |     
   61 |     // Main analysis functions
   62 |     bool AnalyzeWavePattern();
   63 |     bool UpdateCurrentWave();
   64 | 
   65 |     // Data Access
   66 |     SWavePattern* GetContext() { return &m_currentPattern; }
   67 | 
   68 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   69 | //| Orchestrates the entire wave pattern analysis process.         |
   70 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   71 | bool CSonicRWavePattern::AnalyzeWavePattern()
   72 | {
   73 |     if (m_pLogger == NULL)
   74 |     {
   75 |         Print("Logger not initialized in CSonicRWavePattern");
   76 |         return false;
   77 |     }
   78 | 
   79 |     m_pLogger.LogInfo("Starting new wave pattern analysis cycle.");
   80 | 
   81 |     // Step 1: Identify the latest swing points on the chart.
   82 |     if (!IdentifySwingPoints())
   83 |     {
   84 |         m_pLogger.LogWarning("Failed to identify any swing points. Analysis cannot proceed.");
   85 |         return false;
   86 |     }
   87 | 
   88 |     // Step 2: Classify the identified swing points into a wave structure.
   89 |     if (!ClassifyWaves())
   90 |     {
   91 |         m_pLogger.LogWarning("Failed to classify waves from swing points.");
   92 |         return false;
   93 |     }
   94 | 
   95 |     // Step 3: Validate the integrity of the identified impulse and correction patterns.
   96 |     bool isImpulseValid = ValidateImpulsePattern();
   97 |     bool isCorrectionValid = ValidateCorrectionPattern();
   98 | 
   99 |     if (!isImpulseValid && !isCorrectionValid)
  100 |     {
  101 |         m_pLogger.LogInfo("No valid Elliott Wave pattern was identified.");
  102 |         m_currentPattern.patternStrength = 0.0;
  103 |         return false;
  104 |     }
  105 | 
  106 |     // Step 4: Calculate the strength of the overall pattern.
  107 |     m_currentPattern.patternStrength = CalculatePatternStrength();
  108 | 
  109 |     // Step 5: Log the results of the analysis for debugging and verification.
  110 |     LogWaveAnalysis();
  111 | 
  112 |     m_pLogger.LogInfo("Wave pattern analysis cycle completed. Pattern strength: " + DoubleToString(m_currentPattern.patternStrength, 2));
  113 | 
  114 |     return true;
  115 | }
  116 | 
  117 |     bool IdentifySwingPoints();
  118 |     bool ClassifyWaves();
  119 | 
  120 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  121 | //| Classify identified swing points into Elliott Wave patterns.     |
  122 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  123 | bool CSonicRWavePattern::ClassifyWaves()
  124 | {
  125 |     if (m_swingPointCount < 2)
  126 |     {
  127 |         m_pLogger.LogInfo("Not enough swing points to classify waves.");
  128 |         return false;
  129 |     }
  130 | 
  131 |     // Reset current pattern
  132 |     ZeroMemory(m_currentPattern);
  133 |     m_currentPattern.patternStart = m_swingPoints[0].time;
  134 | 
  135 |     // Simple classification: assume the first 5 swings form an impulse pattern
  136 |     // and the next 3 form a correction. This is a major simplification and
  137 |     // would need a much more sophisticated rule engine for real-world use.
  138 | 
  139 |     int waveIndex = 0;
  140 |     for (int i = 0; i < m_swingPointCount - 1 && waveIndex < 13; i++)
  141 |     {
  142 |         SWaveInfo& wave = m_currentPattern.waves[waveIndex];
  143 |         wave.startPoint = m_swingPoints[i];
  144 |         wave.endPoint = m_swingPoints[i + 1];
  145 | 
  146 |         // Basic properties
  147 |         wave.length = MathAbs(wave.endPoint.price - wave.startPoint.price) / m_pSymbolInfo.PipValue();
  148 |         wave.duration = (wave.endPoint.time - wave.startPoint.time) / 60.0; // Duration in minutes
  149 |         wave.isComplete = true; // Assume complete for this analysis
  150 | 
  151 |         // Simplified Type Assignment (needs a proper state machine)
  152 |         if (waveIndex < 5) // First 5 waves are impulse sequence
  153 |         {
  154 |             wave.type = (ENUM_WAVE_TYPE)waveIndex;
  155 |             m_currentPattern.isImpulsePattern = true;
  156 |         }
  157 |         else // Next waves are corrective sequence
  158 |         {
  159 |             wave.type = (ENUM_WAVE_TYPE)(WAVE_CORRECTION_A + (waveIndex - 5));
  160 |             m_currentPattern.isCorrectionPattern = true;
  161 |         }
  162 |         
  163 |         // Calculate Fibonacci ratio relative to the previous wave
  164 |         if (waveIndex > 0)
  165 |         {
  166 |             const SWaveInfo& prevWave = m_currentPattern.waves[waveIndex - 1];
  167 |             if (prevWave.length > 0)
  168 |             {
  169 |                 wave.fibRatio = wave.length / prevWave.length;
  170 |             }
  171 |         }
  172 | 
  173 |         // Calculate individual wave strength
  174 |         wave.strength = CalculateWaveStrength(wave);
  175 |         wave.description = GetWaveTypeString(wave.type);
  176 | 
  177 |         m_currentPattern.waveCount++;
  178 |         waveIndex++;
  179 |     }
  180 | 
  181 |     if (m_currentPattern.waveCount > 0)
  182 |     {
  183 |         m_currentPattern.lastUpdate = TimeCurrent();
  184 |         m_currentPattern.currentWave = m_currentPattern.waves[m_currentPattern.waveCount - 1].type;
  185 |         m_pLogger.LogInfo("Successfully classified " + (string)m_currentPattern.waveCount + " waves.");
  186 |         return true;
  187 |     }
  188 | 
  189 |     return false;
  190 | }
  191 | 
  192 | 
  193 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  194 | //| Identify Swing Points using ZigZag                             |
  195 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  196 | bool CSonicRWavePattern::IdentifySwingPoints()
  197 | {
  198 |     if(m_pIndicators == NULL || m_pSymbolInfo == NULL)
  199 |     {
  200 |         m_pLogger.LogError("Null pointer in IdentifySwingPoints");
  201 |         return false;
  202 |     }
  203 | 
  204 |     // 1. Get ZigZag indicator handle
  205 |     // Note: Parameters (Depth, Deviation, Backstep) should be configurable
  206 |     int zigzag_handle = m_pIndicators.iZigZag(_Symbol, _Period, 12, 5, 3);
  207 |     if(zigzag_handle == INVALID_HANDLE)
  208 |     {
  209 |         m_pLogger.LogError("Failed to create ZigZag indicator handle");
  210 |         return false;
  211 |     }
  212 | 
  213 |     // 2. Prepare arrays to copy ZigZag data
  214 |     double zigzag_buffer[];
  215 |     ArraySetAsSeries(zigzag_buffer, true);
  216 | 
  217 |     // 3. Copy the last N bars of ZigZag data
  218 |     if(CopyBuffer(zigzag_handle, 0, 0, m_lookbackPeriod, zigzag_buffer) <= 0)
  219 |     {
  220 |         m_pLogger.LogWarning("Failed to copy ZigZag buffer data");
  221 |         return false;
  222 |     }
  223 | 
  224 |     // 4. Clear previous swing points
  225 |     ArrayResize(m_swingPoints, 0);
  226 |     m_swingPointCount = 0;
  227 | 
  228 |     // 5. Iterate through ZigZag data to find non-zero values (the swings)
  229 |     for(int i = 0; i < m_lookbackPeriod; i++)
  230 |     {
  231 |         if(zigzag_buffer[i] > 0)
  232 |         {
  233 |             SWavePoint point;
  234 |             point.price = zigzag_buffer[i];
  235 |             point.barIndex = i;
  236 |             
  237 |             // To get the exact time, we need to query the bar's open time
  238 |             datetime times[];
  239 |             if(CopyTime(_Symbol, _Period, i, 1, times) > 0)
  240 |             {
  241 |                 point.time = times[0];
  242 |             }
  243 | 
  244 |             // Determine if it's a high or low swing
  245 |             // This requires comparing with surrounding prices, a simple approximation:
  246 |             double high[], low[];
  247 |             CopyHigh(_Symbol, _Period, i, 1, high);
  248 |             CopyLow(_Symbol, _Period, i, 1, low);
  249 | 
  250 |             if(MathAbs(point.price - high[0]) < m_pSymbolInfo.PipValue())
  251 |             {
  252 |                 point.isHigh = true;
  253 |                 point.isLow = false;
  254 |             }
  255 |             else if(MathAbs(point.price - low[0]) < m_pSymbolInfo.PipValue())
  256 |             {
  257 |                 point.isHigh = false;
  258 |                 point.isLow = true;
  259 |             }
  260 |             
  261 |             // Add to our array of swing points
  262 |             int newSize = m_swingPointCount + 1;
  263 |             ArrayResize(m_swingPoints, newSize);
  264 |             m_swingPoints[m_swingPointCount] = point;
  265 |             m_swingPointCount++;
  266 |         }
  267 |     }
  268 | 
  269 |     // Sort swing points by time ascending
  270 |     // Custom sort function would be needed here if they are not already in order
  271 |     // For now, we assume they are copied in order from oldest to newest.
  272 | 
  273 |     if(m_pLogger != NULL)
  274 |         m_pLogger.LogInfo("Identified " + (string)m_swingPointCount + " swing points.");
  275 | 
  276 |     return m_swingPointCount > 0;
  277 | }
  278 | 
  279 |     
  280 |     // Wave pattern queries
  281 |     bool GetCurrentPattern(SWavePattern& pattern);
  282 |     bool GetWaveInfo(ENUM_WAVE_TYPE waveType, SWaveInfo& waveInfo);
  283 |     int GetSwingPoints(SWavePoint &points[], int count); // New method to get swing points
  284 |     bool IsImpulseWave(ENUM_WAVE_TYPE waveType);
  285 |     bool IsCorrectionWave(ENUM_WAVE_TYPE waveType);
  286 |     
  287 |     // Pattern validation
  288 |     bool ValidateImpulsePattern();
  289 |     bool ValidateCorrectionPattern();
  290 |     bool ValidateFibonacciRatios();
  291 | 
  292 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  293 | //| Validate the impulse wave pattern against Elliott rules.         |
  294 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  295 | bool CSonicRWavePattern::ValidateImpulsePattern()
  296 | {
  297 |     if (!m_currentPattern.isImpulsePattern || m_currentPattern.waveCount < 5)
  298 |         return false;
  299 | 
  300 |     SWaveInfo& w1 = m_currentPattern.waves[0];
  301 |     SWaveInfo& w2 = m_currentPattern.waves[1];
  302 |     SWaveInfo& w3 = m_currentPattern.waves[2];
  303 |     SWaveInfo& w4 = m_currentPattern.waves[3];
  304 |     SWaveInfo& w5 = m_currentPattern.waves[4];
  305 | 
  306 |     // Rule 1: Wave 2 cannot retrace more than 100% of Wave 1.
  307 |     if (w2.endPoint.price <= w1.startPoint.price) // For uptrend
  308 |     {
  309 |         m_pLogger.LogInfo("Impulse Validation Fail: Wave 2 retraced > 100% of Wave 1.");
  310 |         return false;
  311 |     }
  312 | 
  313 |     // Rule 2: Wave 3 can never be the shortest of the impulse waves (1, 3, 5).
  314 |     if (w3.length <= w1.length && w3.length <= w5.length)
  315 |     {
  316 |         m_pLogger.LogInfo("Impulse Validation Fail: Wave 3 is the shortest.");
  317 |         return false;
  318 |     }
  319 | 
  320 |     // Rule 3: Wave 4 does not overlap with the price territory of Wave 1.
  321 |     if (w4.endPoint.price <= w1.endPoint.price) // For uptrend
  322 |     {
  323 |         m_pLogger.LogInfo("Impulse Validation Fail: Wave 4 overlaps Wave 1.");
  324 |         return false;
  325 |     }
  326 | 
  327 |     m_pLogger.LogInfo("Impulse Wave Pattern is valid.");
  328 |     return true;
  329 | }
  330 | 
  331 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  332 | //| Validate the correction wave pattern against Elliott rules.      |
  333 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  334 | bool CSonicRWavePattern::ValidateCorrectionPattern()
  335 | {
  336 |     if (!m_currentPattern.isCorrectionPattern || m_currentPattern.waveCount < 8)
  337 |         return false; // Assumes an ABC correction after a 5-wave impulse
  338 | 
  339 |     SWaveInfo& wA = m_currentPattern.waves[5];
  340 |     SWaveInfo& wB = m_currentPattern.waves[6];
  341 |     SWaveInfo& wC = m_currentPattern.waves[7];
  342 | 
  343 |     // Basic ZigZag (5-3-5) validation (simplified)
  344 |     // A proper validation would need to check the sub-waves of A, B, and C.
  345 |     
  346 |     // Guideline: Wave B typically retraces 38.2% to 61.8% of Wave A.
  347 |     double retracement = wB.length / wA.length;
  348 |     if (retracement < 0.382 - m_fibTolerance || retracement > 0.618 + m_fibTolerance)
  349 |     {
  350 |         m_pLogger.LogInfo("Correction Guideline Fail: Wave B retracement is outside typical range.");
  351 |         // This is a guideline, not a strict rule, so we don't return false.
  352 |     }
  353 | 
  354 |     m_pLogger.LogInfo("Correction Wave Pattern is valid (basic check).");
  355 |     return true;
  356 | }
  357 | 
  358 |     
  359 |     // Strength calculations
  360 |     double CalculateWaveStrength(const SWaveInfo& wave);
  361 |     double CalculatePatternStrength();
  362 |     double GetOverallStrength() { return m_currentPattern.patternStrength; }
  363 |     
  364 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  365 | //| Calculate Wave Strength based on multiple factors               |
  366 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  367 | double CSonicRWavePattern::CalculateWaveStrength(const SWaveInfo& wave)
  368 | {
  369 |     if(!wave.isComplete) return 0.0;
  370 |     
  371 |     double strength = 0.0;
  372 |     double weightSum = 0.0;
  373 |     
  374 |     // 1. Length Factor (20% weight)
  375 |     const double LENGTH_WEIGHT = 0.20;
  376 |     double lengthScore = 0.0;
  377 |     
  378 |     // Compare wave length to average wave length
  379 |     double avgLength = 0.0;
  380 |     int validWaves = 0;
  381 |     for(int i = 0; i < m_currentPattern.waveCount; i++)
  382 |     {
  383 |         if(m_currentPattern.waves[i].isComplete)
  384 |         {
  385 |             avgLength += m_currentPattern.waves[i].length;
  386 |             validWaves++;
  387 |         }
  388 |     }
  389 |     if(validWaves > 0)
  390 |     {
  391 |         avgLength /= validWaves;
  392 |         lengthScore = wave.length > avgLength ? 1.0 : wave.length / avgLength;
  393 |     }
  394 |     strength += lengthScore * LENGTH_WEIGHT;
  395 |     weightSum += LENGTH_WEIGHT;
  396 |     
  397 |     // 2. Fibonacci Relationship (30% weight)
  398 |     const double FIB_WEIGHT = 0.30;
  399 |     double fibScore = 0.0;
  400 |     
  401 |     // Check if wave length matches common Fibonacci ratios (0.382, 0.618, 1.0, 1.618, 2.618)
  402 |     double fibLevels[] = {0.382, 0.618, 1.0, 1.618, 2.618};
  403 |     double minFibDiff = 1.0;
  404 |     
  405 |     for(int i = 0; i < ArraySize(fibLevels); i++)
  406 |     {
  407 |         double diff = MathAbs(wave.fibRatio - fibLevels[i]);
  408 |         if(diff < minFibDiff)
  409 |             minFibDiff = diff;
  410 |     }
  411 |     
  412 |     fibScore = 1.0 - MathMin(minFibDiff, 1.0);
  413 |     strength += fibScore * FIB_WEIGHT;
  414 |     weightSum += FIB_WEIGHT;
  415 |     
  416 |     // 3. Volume Analysis (25% weight)
  417 |     const double VOLUME_WEIGHT = 0.25;
  418 |     double volumeScore = 0.0;
  419 |     
  420 |     // Get volume data for wave duration
  421 |     long volumes[];
  422 |     if(CopyTickVolume(_Symbol, _Period, wave.startPoint.barIndex, 
  423 |                      wave.endPoint.barIndex - wave.startPoint.barIndex + 1, volumes) > 0)
  424 |     {
  425 |         // Calculate average volume for the wave period
  426 |         double avgVolume = 0;
  427 |         for(int i = 0; i < ArraySize(volumes); i++)
  428 |             avgVolume += volumes[i];
  429 |         avgVolume /= ArraySize(volumes);
  430 |         
  431 |         // Compare to recent market volume (last 20 bars)
  432 |         long recentVolumes[];
  433 |         if(CopyTickVolume(_Symbol, _Period, 0, 20, recentVolumes) > 0)
  434 |         {
  435 |             double recentAvgVolume = 0;
  436 |             for(int i = 0; i < ArraySize(recentVolumes); i++)
  437 |                 recentAvgVolume += recentVolumes[i];
  438 |             recentAvgVolume /= ArraySize(recentVolumes);
  439 |             
  440 |             volumeScore = avgVolume > recentAvgVolume ? 1.0 : avgVolume / recentAvgVolume;
  441 |         }
  442 |     }
  443 |     strength += volumeScore * VOLUME_WEIGHT;
  444 |     weightSum += VOLUME_WEIGHT;
  445 |     
  446 |     // 4. Time Factor (15% weight)
  447 |     const double TIME_WEIGHT = 0.15;
  448 |     double timeScore = 0.0;
  449 |     
  450 |     // Check if wave duration is within reasonable bounds
  451 |     // Too short or too long waves are less reliable
  452 |     const int MIN_WAVE_BARS = 3;
  453 |     const int MAX_WAVE_BARS = 55; // Approximately 1 month on H1
  454 |     
  455 |     int waveBars = wave.endPoint.barIndex - wave.startPoint.barIndex;
  456 |     if(waveBars >= MIN_WAVE_BARS && waveBars <= MAX_WAVE_BARS)
  457 |     {
  458 |         timeScore = 1.0 - (double)(waveBars - MIN_WAVE_BARS) / (MAX_WAVE_BARS - MIN_WAVE_BARS);
  459 |     }
  460 |     strength += timeScore * TIME_WEIGHT;
  461 |     weightSum += TIME_WEIGHT;
  462 |     
  463 |     // 5. Pattern Context (10% weight)
  464 |     const double CONTEXT_WEIGHT = 0.10;
  465 |     double contextScore = 0.0;
  466 |     
  467 |     // Check if wave fits expected pattern sequence
  468 |     if(IsImpulseWave(wave.type))
  469 |     {
  470 |         // Impulse waves should be stronger in direction of trend
  471 |         if(wave.type == WAVE_IMPULSE_3) // Wave 3 should be strongest
  472 |             contextScore = 1.0;
  473 |         else if(wave.type == WAVE_IMPULSE_5) // Wave 5 often shows divergence
  474 |             contextScore = 0.7;
  475 |         else
  476 |             contextScore = 0.85;
  477 |     }
  478 |     else if(IsCorrectionWave(wave.type))
  479 |     {
  480 |         // Corrections should be weaker than impulses
  481 |         contextScore = 0.6;
  482 |     }
  483 |     
  484 |     strength += contextScore * CONTEXT_WEIGHT;
  485 |     weightSum += CONTEXT_WEIGHT;
  486 |     
  487 |     // Normalize final strength value
  488 |     if(weightSum > 0)
  489 |         strength /= weightSum;
  490 |         
  491 |     return MathMin(MathMax(strength, 0.0), 1.0); // Ensure result is between 0 and 1
  492 | }
  493 | 
  494 |     
  495 |     // Fibonacci analysis
  496 |     bool CalculateFibonacciLevels(const SWavePoint& start, const SWavePoint& end, double &levels[]);
  497 |     bool IsPriceAtFibLevel(double price, double tolerance = 0.0);
  498 |     double GetNearestFibLevel(double price);
  499 |     
  500 |     // Prediction functions
  501 |     bool PredictNextWave(SWaveInfo& predictedWave);
  502 |     bool GetWaveTargets(ENUM_WAVE_TYPE waveType, double &targets[]);
  503 |     
  504 |     // Utility functions
  505 |     string GetWaveTypeString(ENUM_WAVE_TYPE type);
  506 |     string GetWaveDegreeString(ENUM_WAVE_DEGREE degree);
  507 |     void LogWaveAnalysis();
  508 | 
  509 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  510 | //| Calculate the overall strength of the identified pattern.        |
  511 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  512 | double CSonicRWavePattern::CalculatePatternStrength()
  513 | {
  514 |     if (m_currentPattern.waveCount == 0) return 0.0;
  515 | 
  516 |     double totalStrength = 0;
  517 |     double totalWeight = 0;
  518 | 
  519 |     for (int i = 0; i < m_currentPattern.waveCount; i++)
  520 |     {
  521 |         SWaveInfo& wave = m_currentPattern.waves[i];
  522 |         double weight = 1.0; // Default weight
  523 | 
  524 |         // Assign higher weight to more significant waves
  525 |         if (wave.type == WAVE_IMPULSE_3) weight = 1.5;
  526 |         if (wave.type == WAVE_IMPULSE_5) weight = 1.2;
  527 | 
  528 |         totalStrength += wave.strength * weight;
  529 |         totalWeight += weight;
  530 |     }
  531 | 
  532 |     if (totalWeight == 0) return 0.0;
  533 | 
  534 |     return totalStrength / totalWeight;
  535 | }
  536 | 
  537 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  538 | //| Converts a wave type enum to its string representation.          |
  539 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  540 | string CSonicRWavePattern::GetWaveTypeString(ENUM_WAVE_TYPE type)
  541 | {
  542 |     switch (type)
  543 |     {
  544 |         case WAVE_IMPULSE_1: return "Impulse 1";
  545 |         case WAVE_IMPULSE_2: return "Impulse 2";
  546 |         case WAVE_IMPULSE_3: return "Impulse 3";
  547 |         case WAVE_IMPULSE_4: return "Impulse 4";
  548 |         case WAVE_IMPULSE_5: return "Impulse 5";
  549 |         case WAVE_CORRECTION_A: return "Correction A";
  550 |         case WAVE_CORRECTION_B: return "Correction B";
  551 |         case WAVE_CORRECTION_C: return "Correction C";
  552 |         default: return "Unknown";
  553 |     }
  554 | }
  555 | 
  556 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  557 | //| Logs the details of the current wave pattern analysis.           |
  558 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  559 | void CSonicRWavePattern::LogWaveAnalysis()
  560 | {
  561 |     if (m_pLogger == NULL || m_currentPattern.waveCount == 0) return;
  562 | 
  563 |     m_pLogger.LogInfo("--- Wave Pattern Analysis Report ---");
  564 |     m_pLogger.LogInfo("Identified Pattern Strength: " + DoubleToString(m_currentPattern.patternStrength, 2));
  565 |     m_pLogger.LogInfo("Total Waves: " + (string)m_currentPattern.waveCount);
  566 | 
  567 |     for (int i = 0; i < m_currentPattern.waveCount; i++)
  568 |     {
  569 |         SWaveInfo& wave = m_currentPattern.waves[i];
  570 |         string log = StringFormat("  Wave %s: Start(%.5f @ %s) -> End(%.5f @ %s), Length: %.1f pips, Strength: %.2f, Fib: %.3f",
  571 |                                   wave.description,
  572 |                                   wave.startPoint.price,
  573 |                                   TimeToString(wave.startPoint.time),
  574 |                                   wave.endPoint.price,
  575 |                                   TimeToString(wave.endPoint.time),
  576 |                                   wave.length,
  577 |                                   wave.strength,
  578 |                                   wave.fibRatio);
  579 |         m_pLogger.LogInfo(log);
  580 |     }
  581 |     m_pLogger.LogInfo("--- End of Report ---");
  582 | }
  583 | 
  584 | };
  585 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  586 | //| Constructor                                                      |
  587 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  588 | CSonicRWavePattern::CSonicRWavePattern() : m_pLogger(NULL),
  589 |                                            m_pIndicators(NULL),
  590 |                                            m_pSymbolInfo(NULL),
  591 |                                            m_lookbackPeriod(200),
  592 |                                            m_minWaveLength(5),
  593 |                                            m_fibTolerance(0.05),
  594 |                                            m_strengthThreshold(0.5),
  595 |                                            m_swingPointCount(0)
  596 | {
  597 |     ArrayInitialize(m_fibLevels, 0.0);
  598 | }
  599 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  600 | //| Destructor                                                       |
  601 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  602 | CSonicRWavePattern::~CSonicRWavePattern()
  603 | {
  604 |     Deinitialize();
  605 | }
  606 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  607 | //| Initialize the wave pattern analyzer
  608 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  609 | bool CSonicRWavePattern::Initialize(CLogger* pLogger, CIndicators* pIndicators, CSymbolInfo* pSymbolInfo)
  610 | {
  611 |     m_pLogger = pLogger;
  612 |     m_pIndicators = pIndicators;
  613 |     m_pSymbolInfo = pSymbolInfo;
  614 | 
  615 |     if(m_pLogger == NULL || m_pIndicators == NULL || m_pSymbolInfo == NULL)
  616 |     {
  617 |         Print("ERROR: CSonicRWavePattern::Initialize - Null dependency provided.");
  618 |         return false;
  619 |     }
  620 | 
  621 |     ArrayResize(m_swingPoints, 0);
  622 |     m_swingPointCount = 0;
  623 | 
  624 |     LOG_INFO("CSonicRWavePattern initialized successfully.");
  625 |     return true;
  626 | }
  627 | 
  628 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  629 | //| Deinitialize the wave pattern analyzer
  630 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  631 | void CSonicRWavePattern::Deinitialize()
  632 | {
  633 |     if(m_pLogger)
  634 |         LOG_INFO("Deinitializing CSonicRWavePattern.");
  635 |     
  636 |     ArrayFree(m_swingPoints);
  637 |     m_swingPointCount = 0;
  638 | }
  639 | 
  640 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  641 | //| Main analysis function to coordinate the process
  642 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  643 | bool CSonicRWavePattern::AnalyzeWavePattern()
  644 | {
  645 |     if(!IdentifySwingPoints())
  646 |     {
  647 |         LOG_WARN(*m_pLogger, __FUNCTION__, "Failed to identify swing points. Wave analysis aborted.");
  648 |         return false;
  649 |     }
  650 | 
  651 |     if(!ClassifyWaves())
  652 |     {
  653 |         LOG_WARN(*m_pLogger, __FUNCTION__, "Failed to classify waves from swing points.");
  654 |         return false;
  655 |     }
  656 | 
  657 |     LOG_INFO("Wave pattern analysis completed successfully.");
  658 |     return true;
  659 | }
  660 | 
  661 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  662 | //| Identify swing points using the ZigZag indicator
  663 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  664 | bool CSonicRWavePattern::IdentifySwingPoints()
  665 | {
  666 |     if(m_pIndicators == NULL || m_pSymbolInfo == NULL)
  667 |         return false;
  668 | 
  669 |     // ZigZag parameters - these could be configurable
  670 |     int ExtDepth = 12;
  671 |     int ExtDeviation = 5;
  672 |     int ExtBackstep = 3;
  673 | 
  674 |     // Define the ZigZag indicator
  675 |     MqlIndicator anIndicator;
  676 |     anIndicator.name = "ZigZag";
  677 |     anIndicator.type = IND_CUSTOM;
  678 |     anIndicator.symbol = m_pSymbolInfo.Symbol();
  679 |     anIndicator.period = m_pSymbolInfo.Timeframe();
  680 |     anIndicator.parameters[0].type = TYPE_INT;
  681 |     anIndicator.parameters[0].integer_value = ExtDepth;
  682 |     anIndicator.parameters[1].type = TYPE_INT;
  683 |     anIndicator.parameters[1].integer_value = ExtDeviation;
  684 |     anIndicator.parameters[2].type = TYPE_INT;
  685 |     anIndicator.parameters[2].integer_value = ExtBackstep;
  686 |     anIndicator.num_parameters = 3;
  687 | 
  688 |     int handle = m_pIndicators.GetIndicatorHandle(anIndicator);
  689 |     if(handle == INVALID_HANDLE)
  690 |     {
  691 |         LOG_ERROR("Failed to get ZigZag indicator handle.");
  692 |         return false;
  693 |     }
  694 | 
  695 |     // Reset swing points
  696 |     ArrayResize(m_swingPoints, 0);
  697 |     m_swingPointCount = 0;
  698 | 
  699 |     double zigzagBuffer[];
  700 |     if(CopyBuffer(handle, 0, 0, m_lookbackPeriod, zigzagBuffer) <= 0)
  701 |     {
  702 |         LOG_ERROR("Failed to copy ZigZag buffer data.");
  703 |         return false;
  704 |     }
  705 | 
  706 |     MqlRates rates[];
  707 |     if(CopyRates(m_pSymbolInfo.Symbol(), m_pSymbolInfo.Timeframe(), 0, m_lookbackPeriod, rates) <= 0)
  708 |     {
  709 |         LOG_ERROR("Failed to copy rates for swing point analysis.");
  710 |         return false;
  711 |     }
  712 | 
  713 |     // Iterate backwards to find swing points
  714 |     for(int i = m_lookbackPeriod - 1; i >= 0; i--)
  715 |     {
  716 |         if(zigzagBuffer[i] > 0)
  717 |         {
  718 |             SWavePoint point;
  719 |             point.price = zigzagBuffer[i];
  720 |             point.time = rates[i].time;
  721 |             point.barIndex = i;
  722 |             point.isHigh = (point.price == rates[i].high);
  723 |             point.isLow = (point.price == rates[i].low);
  724 |             point.strength = 1.0; // Placeholder strength
  725 | 
  726 |             // Add to our array if it's a new point
  727 |             if(m_swingPointCount == 0 || m_swingPoints[m_swingPointCount - 1].price != point.price)
  728 |             {
  729 |                 if(ArrayResize(m_swingPoints, m_swingPointCount + 1) > 0)
  730 |                 {
  731 |                     m_swingPoints[m_swingPointCount] = point;
  732 |                     m_swingPointCount++;
  733 |                 }
  734 |             }
  735 |         }
  736 |     }
  737 | 
  738 |     if(m_swingPointCount < 2)
  739 |     {
  740 |         LOG_INFO("Not enough swing points identified (" + (string)m_swingPointCount + ").");
  741 |         return false;
  742 |     }
  743 | 
  744 |     LOG_INFO("Identified " + (string)m_swingPointCount + " swing points.");
  745 |     return true;
  746 | }
  747 | 
  748 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  749 | //| Classify waves based on the identified swing points
  750 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  751 | bool CSonicRWavePattern::ClassifyWaves()
  752 | {
  753 |     if(m_swingPointCount < 6) // Need at least 6 points for a 5-wave pattern (0-1-2-3-4-5)
  754 |     {
  755 |         LOG_INFO("Insufficient swing points to classify a 5-wave pattern.");
  756 |         return false;
  757 |     }
  758 | 
  759 |     // --- Basic 5-Wave Impulse Pattern Recognition (Bullish Example) ---
  760 |     // This is a simplified starting point. A robust implementation is far more complex.
  761 | 
  762 |     // We iterate backwards through the swing points
  763 |     for(int i = m_swingPointCount - 1; i >= 5; i--)
  764 |     {
  765 |         SWavePoint p5 = m_swingPoints[i];     // Potential Wave 5 end
  766 |         SWavePoint p4 = m_swingPoints[i-1];   // Potential Wave 4 end
  767 |         SWavePoint p3 = m_swingPoints[i-2];   // Potential Wave 3 end
  768 |         SWavePoint p2 = m_swingPoints[i-3];   // Potential Wave 2 end
  769 |         SWavePoint p1 = m_swingPoints[i-4];   // Potential Wave 1 end
  770 |         SWavePoint p0 = m_swingPoints[i-5];   // Potential Wave 0 start
  771 | 
  772 |         // Rule 1: Wave 2 cannot retrace more than 100% of Wave 1.
  773 |         bool rule1 = (p2.price > p0.price);
  774 | 
  775 |         // Rule 2: Wave 3 cannot be the shortest of the impulse waves (1, 3, 5).
  776 |         double len1 = MathAbs(p1.price - p0.price);
  777 |         double len3 = MathAbs(p3.price - p2.price);
  778 |         double len5 = MathAbs(p5.price - p4.price);
  779 |         bool rule2 = (len3 > len1 && len3 > len5);
  780 | 
  781 |         // Rule 3: Wave 4 does not overlap with the price territory of Wave 1.
  782 |         bool rule3 = (p4.price > p1.price);
  783 | 
  784 |         if(rule1 && rule2 && rule3)
  785 |         {
  786 |             LOG_INFO("Potential bullish 5-wave impulse pattern found ending at bar " + (string)p5.barIndex);
  787 |             // Here you would populate the m_currentPattern struct
  788 |             // For now, we just log and return true
  789 |             return true;
  790 |         }
  791 |     }
  792 | 
  793 |     LOG_INFO("No clear 5-wave impulse pattern found in the current set of swing points.");
  794 |         return false; // No pattern found that matches the rules
  795 | }
  796 | 
  797 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  798 | //| Get recent swing points                                          |
  799 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  800 | int CSonicRWavePattern::GetSwingPoints(SWavePoint &points[], int count)
  801 | {
  802 |     int pointsToCopy = MathMin(count, m_swingPointCount);
  803 |     if(pointsToCopy <= 0)
  804 |         return 0;
  805 | 
  806 |     // Copy the most recent swing points
  807 |     if(ArrayCopy(points, m_swingPoints, 0, m_swingPointCount - pointsToCopy, pointsToCopy) > 0)
  808 |     {
  809 |         ArraySetAsSeries(points, true); // Ensure the copied array is in series order
  810 |         return pointsToCopy;
  811 |     }
  812 | 
  813 |     return 0;
  814 | }
  815 | 
  816 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  817 | //| Log the current wave analysis results                            |
  818 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  819 | void CSonicRWavePattern::LogWaveAnalysis()
  820 | {
  821 |     if(!m_pLogger) return;
  822 | 
  823 |     LOG_INFO("--- Wave Analysis Report ---");
  824 |     for(int i = 0; i < m_currentPattern.waveCount; i++)
  825 |     {
  826 |         SWaveInfo &wave = m_currentPattern.waves[i];
  827 |         string msg = StringFormat("Wave %s: Type=%s, Strength=%.2f, Length=%.1f pips, Duration=%.0f mins",
  828 |                                   wave.description, GetWaveTypeString(wave.type), wave.strength, wave.length, wave.duration);
  829 |         LOG_INFO(msg);
  830 |     }
  831 |     LOG_INFO("--------------------------");
  832 | }
  833 | 
  834 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  835 | //| GetWaveTypeString (Utility)                                      |
  836 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  837 | string CSonicRWavePattern::GetWaveTypeString(ENUM_WAVE_TYPE type)
  838 | {
  839 |     switch(type)
  840 |     {
  841 |         case WAVE_IMPULSE_1: return "Impulse 1";
  842 |         case WAVE_IMPULSE_2: return "Impulse 2";
  843 |         case WAVE_IMPULSE_3: return "Impulse 3";
  844 |         case WAVE_IMPULSE_4: return "Impulse 4";
  845 |         case WAVE_IMPULSE_5: return "Impulse 5";
  846 |         case WAVE_CORRECTION_A: return "Correction A";
  847 |         case WAVE_CORRECTION_B: return "Correction B";
  848 |         case WAVE_CORRECTION_C: return "Correction C";
  849 |         case WAVE_TRIANGLE: return "Triangle";
  850 |         case WAVE_FLAT: return "Flat";
  851 |         case WAVE_COMPLEX: return "Complex";
  852 |         default: return "Unknown";
  853 |     }
  854 | }
  855 | 
  856 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  857 | //| GetWaveDegreeString (Utility)                                    |
  858 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  859 | string CSonicRWavePattern::GetWaveDegreeString(ENUM_WAVE_DEGREE degree)
  860 | {
  861 |     switch(degree)
  862 |     {
  863 |         case DEGREE_GRAND_SUPERCYCLE: return "Grand Supercycle";
  864 |         case DEGREE_SUPERCYCLE: return "Supercycle";
  865 |         case DEGREE_CYCLE: return "Cycle";
  866 |         case DEGREE_PRIMARY: return "Primary";
  867 |         case DEGREE_INTERMEDIATE: return "Intermediate";
  868 |         case DEGREE_MINOR: return "Minor";
  869 |         case DEGREE_MINUTE: return "Minute";
  870 |         case DEGREE_MINUETTE: return "Minuette";
  871 |         case DEGREE_SUBMINUETTE: return "Subminuette";
  872 |         default: return "Unknown";
  873 |     }
  874 | }

рҹ”— ДҗЖ°б»қng dбә«n Д‘бә§y Д‘б»§: C:\Users\ADMIN\AppData\Roaming\MetaQuotes\Terminal\D0E8209F77C8CF37AD8BF550E51FF075\MQL5\Experts\Sonic R_MC\01_SONIC R_MC_FINAL\Core_Core.mqh

рҹ“Ҡ Thб»‘ng kГӘ: 101 dГІng, 3300 kГҪ tб»ұ, encoding: utf-8

    1 | #ifndef CORE_CORE_MQH
    2 | #define CORE_CORE_MQH
    3 | 
    4 | #include "Core_Context.mqh"
    5 | 
    6 | namespace ApexSonicR 
    7 | {
    8 | 
    9 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   10 | //| CCore                                                            |
   11 | //| The main engine of the EA. Manages the lifecycle of all          |
   12 | //| services and orchestrates the main event handlers.               |
   13 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   14 | class CCore
   15 | {
   16 | private:
   17 |     CEaContext m_Context; // The single source of truth
   18 | 
   19 | public:
   20 |     CCore() {}
   21 |     ~CCore() {}
   22 | 
   23 |     bool Initialize();
   24 |     void Deinitialize();
   25 |     void OnTick();
   26 |     void OnTimer();
   27 |     void OnChartEvent(const int id, const long &lparam, const double &dparam, const string &sparam);
   28 | 
   29 |     // Getter for the context, allows external components to access services
   30 |     CEaContext* GetContext() { return &m_Context; }
   31 | };
   32 | 
   33 | bool CCore::Initialize()
   34 | {
   35 |     //--- 1. Instantiate Services ---
   36 |     m_Context.pLogger       = new CLogger();
   37 |     m_Context.pErrorHandler = new CErrorHandler();
   38 |     m_Context.pSymbolInfo   = new CSymbolInfo();
   39 |     m_Context.pTimeManager  = new CTimeManager();
   40 | 
   41 |     //--- 2. Initialize Services (in dependency order) ---
   42 |     // Logger and ErrorHandler have no dependencies on other services, but need the context itself.
   43 |     if(!m_Context.pLogger->Initialize(&m_Context)) return false;
   44 |     if(!m_Context.pErrorHandler->Initialize(&m_Context)) return false;
   45 | 
   46 |     m_Context.pLogger->Log(LOG_INFO, "Core services instantiated. Initializing...");
   47 | 
   48 |     // SymbolInfo and TimeManager depend on the context.
   49 |     if(!m_Context.pSymbolInfo->Initialize(&m_Context))
   50 |     {
   51 |         m_Context.pErrorHandler->HandleError(0, "CCore::Initialize", "Failed to initialize SymbolInfo");
   52 |         return false;
   53 |     }
   54 |     if(!m_Context.pTimeManager->Initialize(&m_Context))
   55 |     {
   56 |         m_Context.pErrorHandler->HandleError(0, "CCore::Initialize", "Failed to initialize TimeManager");
   57 |         return false;
   58 |     }
   59 | 
   60 |     m_Context.pLogger->Log(LOG_INFO, "All core services initialized successfully.");
   61 |     return true;
   62 | }
   63 | 
   64 | void CCore::Deinitialize()
   65 | {
   66 |     // Deinitialize in reverse order of initialization
   67 |     if(m_Context.pTimeManager)  { m_Context.pTimeManager->Deinitialize();  delete m_Context.pTimeManager;  }
   68 |     if(m_Context.pSymbolInfo)   { m_Context.pSymbolInfo->Deinitialize();   delete m_Context.pSymbolInfo;   }
   69 |     if(m_Context.pErrorHandler) { m_Context.pErrorHandler->Deinitialize(); delete m_Context.pErrorHandler; }
   70 |     if(m_Context.pLogger)       { m_Context.pLogger->Deinitialize();       delete m_Context.pLogger;       }
   71 |     
   72 |     Print("Core services deinitialized.");
   73 | }
   74 | 
   75 | void CCore::OnTick()
   76 | {
   77 |     // Update services that need tick-by-tick data
   78 |     if(m_Context.pSymbolInfo) m_Context.pSymbolInfo->OnTick();
   79 |     if(m_Context.pTimeManager) m_Context.pTimeManager->OnTick();
   80 | 
   81 |     // --- Main EA Logic would be triggered from here ---
   82 |     // Example:
   83 |     // if(m_Context.IsNewBarEvent)
   84 |     // {
   85 |     //     m_Context.pSignalEngine->Run();
   86 |     // }
   87 | }
   88 | 
   89 | void CCore::OnTimer()
   90 | {
   91 |     // Handle timer events, e.g., for UI updates
   92 | }
   93 | 
   94 | void CCore::OnChartEvent(const int id, const long &lparam, const double &dparam, const string &sparam)
   95 | {
   96 |     // Handle chart events
   97 | }
   98 | 
   99 | } // namespace ApexSonicR
  100 | 
  101 | #endif // CORE_CORE_MQH
================================================================================

  344 | #define SAFE_ARRAY_DELETE(ptr)  if(ptr != NULL) { delete[] ptr; ptr = NULL; }
  345 | #define SAFE_RELEASE(handle)    if(handle != INVALID_HANDLE) { IndicatorRelease(handle); handle = INVALID_HANDLE; }
  346 | #define IS_VALID_POINTER(ptr)   (ptr != NULL)
  347 | #define NORMALIZE_PRICE(price)  NormalizeDouble(price, _Digits)
  348 | #define NORMALIZE_LOT(lot)      NormalizeDouble(lot, 2)
  349 | #define NORMALIZE_VOLUME(volume) NormalizeDouble(volume, 2)
  350 | #define POINTS_TO_PRICE(points) (points * _Point)
  351 | #define PRICE_TO_POINTS(price)  (int)(price / _Point)
  352 | 
  353 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  354 | //| LOGGING MACROS (RENAMED TO AVOID CONFLICTS)                     |
  355 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  356 | #define APEX_LOG_ERROR(msg) \
  357 |     Print("ERROR [" + __FUNCTION__ + "]: " + msg);
  358 | 
  359 | #define APEX_LOG_WARNING(msg) \
  360 |     Print("WARNING [" + __FUNCTION__ + "]: " + msg);
  361 | 
  362 | #define APEX_LOG_INFO(msg) \
  363 |     Print("INFO [" + __FUNCTION__ + "]: " + msg);
  364 | 
  365 | #define APEX_LOG_DEBUG(msg) \
  366 |     Print("DEBUG [" + __FUNCTION__ + "]: " + msg);
  367 | 
  368 | // Legacy compatibility (keep old names but redirect)
  369 | #define LOG_ERROR(msg)   APEX_LOG_ERROR(msg)
  370 | #define LOG_WARNING(msg) APEX_LOG_WARNING(msg) 
  371 | #define LOG_INFO(msg)    APEX_LOG_INFO(msg)
  372 | #define LOG_DEBUG(msg)   APEX_LOG_DEBUG(msg)
  373 | 
  374 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  375 | //| ERROR HANDLING                                                   |
  376 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  377 | #define CHECK_POINTER(ptr, msg) \
  378 |     if(ptr == NULL) { \
  379 |         Print("ERROR: " + msg + " - Null pointer"); \
  380 |         return false; \
  381 |     }
  382 | 
  383 | #define CHECK_INIT(result, msg) \
  384 |     if(!result) { \
  385 |         Print("ERROR: " + msg + " - Initialization failed"); \
  386 |         return false; \
  387 |     }
  388 | 
  389 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  390 | //| VALIDATION FUNCTIONS                                             |
  391 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  392 | bool IsValidPrice(double price) {
  393 |     return (price > 0.0 && price != EMPTY_VALUE);
  394 | }
  395 | 
  396 | bool IsValidVolume(double volume) {
  397 |     return (volume >= MIN_LOT_SIZE && volume <= MAX_LOT_SIZE);
  398 | }
  399 | 
  400 | bool IsValidStopLevel(double entry, double stop) {
  401 |     if(!IsValidPrice(entry) || !IsValidPrice(stop)) return false;
  402 |     double stopLevel = SymbolInfoInteger(_Symbol, SYMBOL_TRADE_STOPS_LEVEL) * _Point;
  403 |     return (MathAbs(entry - stop) >= stopLevel);
  404 | }
  405 | 
  406 | bool IsMarketOpen() {
  407 |     return (SymbolInfoInteger(_Symbol, SYMBOL_TRADE_MODE) == SYMBOL_TRADE_MODE_FULL);
  408 | }
  409 | 
  410 | bool IsNewBar(ENUM_TIMEFRAMES timeframe = PERIOD_CURRENT) {
  411 |     static datetime lastBarTime = 0;
  412 |     datetime currentBarTime = iTime(_Symbol, timeframe, 0);
  413 |     if(currentBarTime != lastBarTime) {
  414 |         lastBarTime = currentBarTime;
  415 |         return true;
  416 |     }
  417 |     return false;
  418 | }
  419 | 
  420 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  421 | //| UTILITY FUNCTIONS                                                |
  422 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  423 | double GetSpreadInPoints() {
  424 |     return (SymbolInfoDouble(_Symbol, SYMBOL_ASK) - SymbolInfoDouble(_Symbol, SYMBOL_BID)) / _Point;
  425 | }
  426 | 
  427 | double GetPipValue() {
  428 |     return SymbolInfoDouble(_Symbol, SYMBOL_TRADE_TICK_VALUE);
  429 | }
  430 | 
  431 | // Removed TimeToString and DoubleToString overrides - use built-in functions
  432 | 
  433 | string IntegerToString(long value) {
  434 |     return ::IntegerToString(value);
  435 | }
  436 | 
  437 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+

   19 | enum ENUM_ERROR_SEVERITY {
   20 |     ERROR_SEVERITY_INFO = 0,        // Informational messages
   21 |     ERROR_SEVERITY_WARNING = 1,     // Warning conditions
   22 |     ERROR_SEVERITY_ERROR = 2,       // Error conditions
   23 |     ERROR_SEVERITY_CRITICAL = 3,    // Critical errors that may stop EA
   24 |     ERROR_SEVERITY_FATAL = 4        // Fatal errors requiring immediate shutdown
   25 | };
   26 | 
   27 | enum ENUM_ERROR_CATEGORY {
   28 |     ERROR_CATEGORY_GENERAL = 0,     // General errors
   29 |     ERROR_CATEGORY_TRADING = 1,     // Trading-related errors
   30 |     ERROR_CATEGORY_MARKET_DATA = 2, // Market data errors
   31 |     ERROR_CATEGORY_BROKER = 3,      // Broker connection errors
   32 |     ERROR_CATEGORY_CALCULATION = 4, // Mathematical calculation errors
   33 |     ERROR_CATEGORY_INITIALIZATION = 5, // Initialization errors
   34 |     ERROR_CATEGORY_MEMORY = 6       // Memory management errors
   35 | };
   36 | 
   37 | // Phase 5.1.2: Enhanced Recovery Enums
   38 | enum ENUM_RECOVERY_ACTION {
   39 |     RECOVERY_NONE = 0,              // No recovery action
   40 |     RECOVERY_RETRY = 1,             // Retry the operation
   41 |     RECOVERY_FALLBACK = 2,          // Use fallback method
   42 |     RECOVERY_RESET = 3,             // Reset component state
   43 |     RECOVERY_RESTART = 4,           // Restart EA component
   44 |     RECOVERY_EMERGENCY_STOP = 5     // Emergency stop EA
   45 | };

   49 |     HEALING_IN_PROGRESS = 1,        // Healing in progress
   50 |     HEALING_SUCCESS = 2,            // Healing successful
   51 |     HEALING_FAILED = 3,             // Healing failed

   55 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   56 | //| Enhanced Error Record Structure                                  |
   57 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   58 | struct ErrorRecord {
   59 |     datetime            timestamp;          // When the error occurred
   60 |     ENUM_ERROR_SEVERITY severity;          // Error severity level
   61 |     ENUM_ERROR_CATEGORY category;          // Error category
   62 |     int                 mql5Code;          // MQL5 error code (0 if not applicable)
   63 |     int                 internalCode;      // Internal application error code
   64 |     string              function;          // Function where error occurred
   65 |     string              module;            // Module where error occurred (NEW)
   66 |     string              message;           // Error message
   67 |     string              stackTrace;        // Stack trace information
   68 |     int                 occurrenceCount;   // How many times this error occurred
   69 |     
   70 |     // Phase 5.1.2: Recovery & Healing
   71 |     ENUM_RECOVERY_ACTION recoveryAction;   // Recommended recovery action
   72 |     ENUM_HEALING_STATUS  healingStatus;    // Auto-healing status
   73 |     int                 retryCount;        // Number of recovery attempts
   74 |     datetime            lastRetryTime;     // Last recovery attempt time
   75 |     bool                isRecoverable;     // Can this error be recovered
   76 |     double              performanceImpact; // Performance impact (0.0-1.0)
   77 |     
   78 |     // Default initialization
   79 |     void Initialize() {
   80 |         timestamp = 0;
   81 |         severity = ERROR_SEVERITY_INFO;
   82 |         category = ERROR_CATEGORY_GENERAL;
   83 |         mql5Code = 0;
   84 |         internalCode = 0;
   85 |         function = "";
   86 |         module = "";
   87 |         message = "";
   88 |         stackTrace = "";
   89 |         occurrenceCount = 1;
   90 |         
   91 |         // Recovery & Healing
   92 |         recoveryAction = RECOVERY_NONE;
   93 |         healingStatus = HEALING_NOT_ATTEMPTED;
   94 |         retryCount = 0;
   95 |         lastRetryTime = 0;
   96 |         isRecoverable = false;
   97 |         performanceImpact = 0.0;
   98 |     }
   99 | };

  101 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  102 | //| Recovery Strategy Structure                                      |
  103 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  104 | struct RecoveryStrategy {
  105 |     ENUM_ERROR_CATEGORY category;
  106 |     ENUM_ERROR_SEVERITY severity;
  107 |     ENUM_RECOVERY_ACTION action;
  108 |     int                 maxRetries;
  109 |     int                 retryDelayMs;
  110 |     bool                enableHealing;
  111 |     string              description;
  112 |     
  113 |     void Initialize() {
  114 |         category = ERROR_CATEGORY_GENERAL;
  115 |         severity = ERROR_SEVERITY_ERROR;
  116 |         action = RECOVERY_RETRY;
  117 |         maxRetries = 3;
  118 |         retryDelayMs = 1000;
  119 |         enableHealing = true;
  120 |         description = "";
  121 |     }
  122 | };
